before: The frequency rule mentions bilateral, extra-adrenal, and metastatic disease before defining these axes.
after: Add a foundation card defining bilateral, extra-adrenal, and metastatic before the frequency summary.
rule_candidate: Introduce unfamiliar classification axes before frequency or comparison cards.

before: Three cards repeat that the same incidental finding needs the same first biochemical check.
after: Keep one diagnosis workflow card and move risk rationale to a separate card with a distinct job.
rule_candidate: Repeated concepts are allowed only when each card tests a different decision point.

before: The patient has episodic symptoms and the next test is {{c1::important}}.
after: The patient has episodic catecholamine-type symptoms, so the first biochemical screen is {{c1::plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines}}.
rule_candidate: Hide the decision-changing test or diagnosis, not a generic importance word.

before: Image caption and explanatory paragraph are adjacent with no spacing, making the card hard to scan.
after: Add spacing between the image cue, caption, and explanation so each block has one visual role.
rule_candidate: Image cards should separate title, image, cue, and explanation blocks.
